title: HEPATOTROPIC VIRUSES (B, D, C): VIROLOGICAL DIAGNOSIS AND PREVENTION
author: Saodat Akhmedova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article provides a scientific overview of the structure, pathogenesis, virological diagnostic methods, and prevention measures for hepatotropic viruses—Hepatitis B (HBV), Hepatitis D (HDV), and Hepatitis C (HCV). The study analyzes modern laboratory methods (ELISA, PCR, serological markers) and prevention strategies in the context of Uzbekistan, based on international and national guidelines.
steps_raw: This article provides a scientific overview of the structure, pathogenesis, virological diagnostic methods, and prevention measures for hepatotropic viruses—Hepatitis B (HBV), Hepatitis D (HDV), and Hepatitis C (HCV). The study analyzes modern laboratory methods (ELISA, PCR, serological markers) and prevention strategies in the context of Uzbekistan, based on international and national guidelines.
